guidance.architecture: Static exact recovery with noise-budget control
guidance.blocker: No structural blocker is present, but the record becomes poorly conditioned as ε shrinks.
guidance.missing: Current record is sufficient for exact recovery on the chosen family.
nextSteps: Keep ε away from zero; the current amplification factor is 4.00.
Use κ(η)/2 to decide whether the current noise budget is acceptable.
If robustness matters more than exactness, redesign the record before changing the protected variable.

guidance.blocker: Phase freedom creates fiber collisions under the fixed-basis record.
guidance.missing: Add at least one complementary measurement basis or restrict the admissible state family to a meridian.

recoverabilityQubit.theoremStatus: Family-specific phase-loss no-go with standard measurement enrichment outside the current theorem spine

failureModes: Phase freedom creates fiber collisions under the fixed-basis record.

recommendations.title: Weaken target to z coordinate only
recommendations.rationale: The fixed-basis record keeps the phase-insensitive z coordinate exact even when the full Bloch vector collapses.

recommendations.title: Add a complementary basis
recommendations.theoremStatus: Standard physics guidance outside the current theorem spine
recommendations.rationale: Adding a complementary basis is the conventional way to recover phase-sensitive information, but this richer architecture is not yet proven in the repo.
recommendations.expectedRegime: likely exact on the enlarged measurement family

chosenRecommendation.title: Weaken target to z coordinate only
chosenRecommendation.rationale: The fixed-basis record keeps the phase-insensitive z coordinate exact even when the full Bloch vector collapses.

comparison.narrative: Weakening the target removes the unresolved protected degrees of freedom that the current record cannot separate.

recoverabilityBoundaryArchitecture.protectedLabel: bounded velocity class with boundary compatibility
recoverabilityBoundaryArchitecture.observationLabel: periodic projector transplanted to a bounded domain
recoverabilityBoundaryArchitecture.classification: Impossible for the strong bounded target under the transplanted architecture

guidance.architecture: Switch to a boundary-compatible bounded-domain architecture
guidance.blocker: The periodic projector removes divergence but leaves the bounded protected class because the projected field picks up the wrong boundary-normal trace.
guidance.missing: Use the restricted boundary-compatible finite-mode Hodge family for exact recovery, or weaken the target to a bulk divergence certificate.
nextSteps: Switch from the periodic transplant to the boundary-compatible Hodge projector on the restricted finite-mode family.
If the application only needs bulk incompressibility and not the full bounded protected class, weaken the target to a divergence certificate.

recoverabilityBoundaryArchitecture.theoremStatus: Theorem-backed bounded-domain transplant failure with restricted exact repair path
recoverabilityBoundaryArchitecture.missingStructure: Use the restricted boundary-compatible finite-mode Hodge family for exact recovery, or weaken the target to a bulk divergence certificate.
recoverabilityBoundaryArchitecture.structuralBlocker: The periodic projector removes divergence but leaves the bounded protected class because the projected field picks up the wrong boundary-normal trace.
recoverabilityBoundaryArchitecture.recommendedArchitecture: Switch to a boundary-compatible bounded-domain architecture
failureModes: The periodic projector removes divergence but leaves the bounded protected class because the projected field picks up the wrong boundary-normal trace.

recommendations.title: Switch to the boundary-compatible Hodge family
recommendations.theoremStatus: Theorem-backed bounded-domain transplant failure with restricted exact repair path
recommendations.rationale: The wrong architecture is the blocker here; the restricted bounded-domain Hodge projector restores exact recovery on the compatible finite-mode family.

recommendations.title: Weaken target to bulk divergence certificate only
recommendations.theoremStatus: Weaker-target fallback using the existing transplanted computation
recommendations.rationale: If the application only needs a divergence certificate, the transplanted architecture can still support that weaker target even though it fails the strong bounded protected class.

chosenRecommendation.title: Switch to the boundary-compatible Hodge family
chosenRecommendation.theoremStatus: Theorem-backed bounded-domain transplant failure with restricted exact repair path
chosenRecommendation.rationale: The wrong architecture is the blocker here; the restricted bounded-domain Hodge projector restores exact recovery on the compatible finite-mode family.

comparison.narrative: The proposed architecture change moves the system into a more appropriate recoverability regime for the chosen record.

nogo.title: Finite-time exact recovery failure for smooth linear flows
nogo.summary: The flow remains invertible at every finite time, so it cannot annihilate a nontrivial disturbance space exactly.

boundaryNoGo.title: Bounded-domain projection transplant failure
boundaryNoGo.summary: The periodic exact projector still removes divergence, but it does not preserve the bounded-domain protected class because the projected field picks up nonzero boundary-normal trace.
